title: Cardiac Diagnostics II — Final Exam (50 Questions)

questions.text: Ultrasound reflection is best defined as:

answers.text: The bending of sound waves as they pass through different media
The absorption of sound energy by dense tissue
The bouncing back of ultrasound waves when they hit a tissue boundary
The conversion of electrical energy into sound waves

questions.text: Ultrasound refraction is best defined as:

answers.text: The bouncing back of sound waves at a tissue interface
The change in direction or bending of sound waves as they pass through media with varying densities
The loss of sound energy due to tissue absorption
The amplification of sound waves by the piezoelectric crystal

questions.text: The component inside an echocardiography transducer that converts electrical energy into sound waves (and vice versa) is the:
For the parasternal echocardiographic window, the patient is positioned in:
The apical echocardiographic window (Point of Maximal Impulse) uses which patient position?

answers.text: Left lateral decubitus or sitting upright

questions.text: Which transducer position requires the patient to be supine and primarily identifies the aortic arch and great vessels?
The subcostal echocardiographic window is performed with the patient in the supine position and is best suited for visualizing the:

answers.text: Left ventricle outflow tract and aortic valve
Inferior vena cava, right atrium, and left atrium
Parasternal long axis of the left ventricle

questions.text: The structures most commonly identified from the parasternal echocardiographic window include:

answers.text: LV, RV, aortic valve, and mitral valve

questions.text: During a transesophageal echocardiogram (TEE), the patient is positioned:

answers.text: Supine with the head of the bed elevated 30°
Right lateral decubitus with knees bent

questions.text: TEE requires which of the following preparations?

answers.text: Full anticoagulation and a Foley catheter
Contrast dye injection and a urinary catheter

questions.text: M-mode echocardiography displays cardiac motion in a one-dimensional format. In this display, what does the horizontal axis represent?
The BART mnemonic in color Doppler echocardiography stands for:
In color Doppler imaging, a GREEN color mosaic most commonly indicates:

answers.text: Normal laminar flow toward the transducer
Normal laminar flow away from the transducer

questions.text: A contrast (bubble) study using agitated saline is performed primarily to identify:
During a bubble study, if bubbles appear in the LEFT atrium shortly after injection into the RIGHT atrium, this finding is most consistent with:
Stress echocardiography is primarily indicated to:

answers.text: Measure pulmonary capillary wedge pressure non-invasively
Diagnose coronary artery disease and assess myocardial ischemia
Quantify ejection fraction at rest only

questions.text: In patients who cannot exercise, pharmacologic stress echocardiography is most commonly performed using:
The primary vascular access site for cardiac catheterization is the:
During cardiac catheterization, the femoral artery is identified by its relationship to which anatomic landmark?
The radial artery is preferred as an alternative access site for cardiac catheterization because it:

answers.text: Provides access to the right coronary artery only
Eliminates the need for a guidewire
Allows for a shorter recovery time

questions.text: During cardiac catheterization, the guidewire is left in place throughout the procedure in order to:

answers.text: Prevent arterial spasm at the access site
Deliver contrast dye directly into the coronary arteries

questions.text: Coronary angiography visualizes the coronary arteries by injecting:
The standard size range for common cardiac catheters is:
The Pigtail catheter is used during cardiac catheterization primarily to image which chamber?
Electrophysiology study (EPS) is best described as:

answers.text: A non-invasive imaging technique using radionuclide isotopes
A diagnostic tool that stimulates or measures cardiac electrical activity
A therapeutic procedure that uses balloon inflation to open coronary arteries
A method of recording cardiac pressures via a Swan-Ganz catheter

questions.text: Cardiac mapping records electrical potentials from the heart in order to assess:

answers.text: Ventricular wall motion and ejection fraction
Blood flow velocity through the coronary arteries

questions.text: Radionuclide studies (blood pool imaging) use a radioactive isotope to measure:

answers.text: Coronary artery anatomy and stenosis severity
Ejection fraction and blood pooling in the heart chambers

questions.text: Which of the following is identified as the MOST COMMON therapeutic cardiac catheterization intervention?
Synchronized cardioversion differs from defibrillation in that it:

answers.text: Delivers an unsynchronized shock at maximum energy
Is used only for pulseless ventricular fibrillation
Delivers a shock timed to the R wave of the QRS complex
Requires no electrode placement on the chest

questions.text: Synchronized cardioversion is the appropriate treatment for which of the following?
Defibrillation delivers an UNSYNCHRONIZED shock and is indicated for:

answers.text: Atrial fibrillation with stable vital signs
Ventricular fibrillation and pulseless ventricular tachycardia

questions.text: Compared to unipolar pacemaker leads, bipolar leads typically provide:

answers.text: Higher voltage shocks to restore sinus rhythm
Longer battery life at the expense of sensing
Reduced capture threshold but inferior sensing

questions.text: Permanent pacemakers are primarily implanted to treat:

answers.text: Atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response

questions.text: VO2 max is most clinically valuable in cardiac rehabilitation because it indicates:

answers.text: The optimal dose of dobutamine for stress testing
The degree of coronary artery stenosis

questions.text: The tilt-table test is primarily used to identify patients with syncope caused by:
During a tilt-table test, if the patient has no reaction at baseline, which drug may be administered to provoke a vasodepressor response?
The three standard indwelling catheters used for hemodynamic monitoring are:

answers.text: A-line, endotracheal tube, and Foley catheter
Arterial line, CVP catheter, and pulmonary artery pressure catheter
CVP catheter, urinary catheter, and arterial line

questions.text: The tip of a central venous pressure (CVP) catheter is positioned in the:
The tip of a pulmonary artery pressure (Swan-Ganz) catheter is positioned in the:
The device that converts catheter-measured intravascular pressures into interpretable numbers on the monitor is the:
In a patient with fluid overload, hemodynamic monitoring values (CVP, PAP, PCWP) would be expected to:
The normal range for mean arterial pressure (MAP) is:
What is the normal range for central venous pressure (CVP)?
What is the normal pulmonary capillary wedge pressure (PCWP)?
The normal range for cardiac output (CO) in an adult at rest is:
The normal range for cardiac index (CI) is:
PCWP is clinically most valuable for differentiating between:

answers.text: Left and right bundle branch block

questions.text: When measuring PCWP with a Swan-Ganz catheter, the balloon must be inflated. After completing the measurement, the balloon should be:

answers.text: Left inflated to maintain a continuous wedge reading
Deflated immediately after the measurement is obtained
Inflated at half pressure to reduce artifact

questions.text: In the thermodilution method for measuring cardiac output, what is injected through the proximal port of the Swan-Ganz catheter?

answers.text: 10 mL of cold saline or D5W

questions.text: The formula for systemic vascular resistance (SVR) is SVR = [(MAP − CVP) ÷ CO] × 80. The factor of 80 is used to convert mmHg into:
